title: Aura Design System
description: An enterprise-grade component library built for rapid UI development, featuring strict accessibility, high customization, and zero-runtime CSS.
problem: Engineers at the firm were spending over 30% of their dev cycles writing custom CSS layouts and fixing accessibility bugs for simple modals, dropdowns, and layouts. The lack of standard components created inconsistent interfaces across products.
solution: Built Aura UI from the ground up, combining Radix UI primitives for perfect WAI-ARIA compliance with Tailwind CSS. Included a custom theme customizer allowing visual generation of theme tokens that map directly to Tailwind configuration files.
features: WAI-ARIA compliant keyboard navigation out of the box
Dynamic dark/light styling via CSS Variables
Framer Motion layout transitions using custom cubic-beziers
Fully documented with interactive code playgrounds
learnings: Radix primitives significantly reduce initial accessibility coding time.
Bundle size optimization is critical for system adoption; we achieved under 12kb minified+gzipped using tree-shaking.

title: Vectra Analytics Dashboard
description: Real-time log monitoring and database metrics visualization platform powered by React and Canvas-based charts, processing millions of events per second.
problem: The original analytics UI suffered major rendering lags when plotting more than 10,000 live data points. Chart updates triggered full React renders, blocking the main thread.
solution: Rebuilt the rendering layer using canvas-optimized charting and windowing algorithms. Implemented custom WebSocket middleware to throttle incoming state updates, ensuring smooth 60fps renders.
features: High-speed canvas graphing engine supporting 50k+ data points
WebSocket-driven log stream with status indicators
learnings: Throttling state updates is crucial in real-time dashboards; UI refreshes at 100ms intervals look identical to real-time but save 90% CPU.
Offloading calculations to Web Workers keeps dashboard interactions perfectly responsive.
metrics: Zero main thread blocking below 50,000 data points
40% drop in user dashboard page load times

title: Pulse Collaborative Editor
description: Collaborative, real-time markdown editor with multi-user cursors, live preview, version history, and decentralized sync options.
problem: Centralized database synchronization for collaborative writing suffers high latency and single points of failure, making offline document editing complex to synchronize.
solution: Built a decentralized collaboration system using conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) with Yjs. Synchronized documents via WebRTC with standard signaling server fallback.
features: Real-time cursor presence showing usernames and color indicators
Offline editing capability with automatic background merging
Markdown parser with real-time WYSIWYG split preview
Version history slider with diff comparisons
learnings: CRDTs solve distributed synchronization reliably but require careful management of delete operations to prevent document bloat.
Optimistic UI updates are necessary to hide transport-layer WebRTC latency.
metrics: Support for up to 100 concurrent editors
